trade.summary: Bilateral trade has been severely reduced following South Africa's severing of diplomatic relations and filing of genocide case at the ICJ. Prior trade included diamonds, chemicals, and agricultural goods. South Africa has implemented trade restrictions and boycotts aligned with its political stance.
disputes: South Africa filed ICJ genocide case against Israel January 2024

diplomatic.summary: South Africa has taken the most aggressive diplomatic stance against Israel of any nation outside the Arab world. South Africa filed a genocide case against Israel at the International Court of Justice in January 2024, withdrew its ambassador, and has called for Israel to be treated as a pariah state. South Africa draws explicit parallels between Israeli policies and apartheid, shaped by the ANC's historical solidarity with the Palestinian cause. Relations are essentially severed.
recent_incidents: South Africa withdrew ambassador from Israel
South Africa called for global arms embargo on Israel

military.summary: No military cooperation. South Africa has called for arms embargoes on Israel and has aligned with Iran and other adversaries of Israel at international forums on security matters.
incidents: South Africa accused Israel of genocide at ICJ

financial.summary: South African pension funds and institutional investors have been under pressure to divest from Israeli assets. Limited bilateral investment. The financial relationship has been largely disrupted by the political crisis.

scientific.summary: Academic boycott campaigns in South Africa have reduced scientific cooperation substantially. Some individual academic contacts continue but institutional cooperation has largely ceased.
